mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate applied to future earnings and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month when policy is not tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not above baseline during flat-to-falling FEDFUNDS periods, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above recent trend, markets infer tighter future policy and a higher real discount rate, which compresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month-over-month or its 3-month annualized change exceeds the prior 12-month average by 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with falling PAYEMS signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, so equity earnings expectations are revised down and S&P 500 returns are negative over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS declines over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint unemployment-payroll deterioration trigger does not produce negative average 20-day returns, or if either proxy alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not correspond to above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO expands while HOUST remains weak, industrial activity is outpacing residential construction and signaling better real activity without a housing overhang, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and HOUST 3-month change is nonpositive
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-up/HOUST-flat-or-down regime is not followed by positive 60-day returns, or if returns are weaker than in periods with neither condition.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 growth and PAYEMS growth signals broad demand slowdown and weakening income support, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1.0% and PAYEMS 3-month average growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if combined weak GDP and payroll growth does not predict negative 60-day returns, or if the signal is not stronger than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE reflects resilient labor demand and durable household spending power, which supports positive equity returns over the next week through reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below 4.2% and unchanged over the latest month
failure_condition: Reject if low-stable unemployment does not correspond to positive 5-day forward returns, or if the signal only works during specific market drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL implies the Fed is behind inflation, raising real yields and compressing valuations, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation state does not predict negative 20-day returns, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for either proxy alone.
